item.title: Olympian David Hearn charged with destruction of property at Reflecting Pool
item.description: David Hearn is accused of ripping out a piece of sealant on the bottom of the Reflecting Pool on June 19, U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro said.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (July 5)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Jane Pauley.

item.title: CBS to host "The Great American Block Party 250" special on July 4
item.description: CBS will host a primetime special celebrating America's 250th birthday on Saturday, July 4, with exclusive performances and the largest fireworks show in history.

item.title: Maps show this July 4th could be hottest ever in parts of U.S.
item.description: Extremely dangerous heat, coupled with humidity, could result in heat index readings of 100 to 115 degrees from the Midwest to the East Coast, forecasters said.

item.title: Scientists create first synthetic cell that can complete life cycle
item.description: University of Minnesota scientists say they've made the first synthetic cell that can complete a life cycle. Kate Adamala, a University of Minnesota professor who worked on the project and the co-founder of Biotic, joined CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Spotify removes streams of No. 1 song after suspicious Kalshi bets
item.description: Spotify said the streams tied to Malcolm Todd's "Earrings" were not from genuine listeners after suspicious betting activity emerged on Kalshi.

item.title: Kennedy Center selling $25,000 ticket package to watch D.C. July 4th fireworks
item.description: The Washington, D.C., fireworks show, which is sponsored by the Trump-backed organization Freedom 250, is not slated to begin until 10:30 p.m. or 11 p.m.

item.title: Olympian David Hearn charged with destruction of property at D.C. Reflecting Pool
item.description: U.S. Attorney Jeanine Pirro on Thursday announced an indictment against Olympian David Hearn on a destruction of property charge related to the D.C. Reflecting Pool. Hearn is accused of ripping out a piece of sealant on the bottom of the Reflecting Pool on June 19, Pirro said.

item.title: Harvard astronomer leading White House UFO council explains what he hopes to uncover
item.description: The White House appointed former Harvard University astronomer Avi Loeb to lead its new panel on Unidentified Anomalous Phenomena, also known as UAPs and UFOs. Loeb shares his enthusiasm for his new role.

item.title: Trump's crypto dealings and potential conflicts of interest
item.description: New financial disclosures show President Trump made $1.4 billion on crypto in 2025. Tom Bergin, investigative financial reporter for Reuters, joins CBS News to discuss the potential conflicts of interest that comes from Mr. Trump's crypto dealings.

item.title: Celeb planners on how MSG could become Taylor Swift's perfect wedding venue
item.description: Two elite event designers shared their thoughts on how Taylor Swift's team might transform Madison Square Garden.

item.title: Takeaways from weaker than expected jobs report
item.description: The U.S. added 57,000 jobs in June, failing to meet the expectations of economists, according to new numbers released from the Bureau of Labor Statistics. CBS News' Javier David and Natalie Brand have more.

item.title: Man who sold land for Kushner-backed Albania resort facing criminal probe
item.description: A man who sold land for a controversial, Jared Kushner-backed luxury development in Albania is suspected of money laundering and drug trafficking.

item.title: U.S.-Iran talks pause for ayatollah's funeral after claims of progress
item.description: Negotiations between Iran and the U.S. will pause for a week for the funeral of Iran's slain supreme leader, but Trump, and mediators, say there's been progress.

item.title: Huge Russian strike on Ukraine's capital kills at least 21, officials say
item.description: Russia launched a massive attack on Ukraine's capital, killing at least 21 people and prompting President Zelenskyy to call for sped-up weapons support from his partners.

item.title: What not to do if your CD account matures this July
item.description: Have a CD account with a July 2026 maturity date? Here are three things to avoid doing before that point.

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce donate $26 million ahead of wedding
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce donated to 20 local and national charities ahead of their wedding Friday.

item.title: Taxes on Trump's $1.4 billion crypto income could total hundreds of millions
item.description: The White House declined to say how much the president paid in taxes on his crypto windfall.

item.title: StubHub accused of failing to deliver pricey World Cup tickets
item.description: Ticket reseller StubHub abruptly canceled customers' tickets to World Cup matches, costing them thousands of dollars, a lawsuit alleges.

item.title: Why tick season is expected to be worse than normal
item.description: The CDC is reporting the highest rate of emergency room visits from tick bites since 2017 in many parts of the U.S.

item.title: Employers added 57,000 jobs in June, far below forecasts
item.description: June's payroll gains were much lower than the 100,000 new hires that economists had predicted.

item.title: 3 futurists predict what America will be like in the centuries ahead
item.description: From space to healthcare and artificial intelligence, what could the next 250 years of the United States look like?

item.title: Who climbed the Empire State Building? Here's what we know.
item.description: The two people taken into custody for climbing New York City's Empire State Building where they apparently got engaged have been identified as Ivan Kuznetsov, 32, and Angela Nikolau, 33.

item.title: Extended interview: Gen Zers on COVID, generational stereotypes, AI and more
item.description: In the last installment of our USA to Z series, Vladimir Duthiers speaks with seven Gen Zers about their generation being next to lead the nation, growing up during the pandemic, stereotypes they see about themselves, the role of AI in their world as they join the workforce and more.

item.title: Taylor Swift's history with MSG, from talent competition to wedding bash
item.description: Taylor Swift has had a decadeslong history with Madison Square Garden, performing there as early as 2003 and even celebrating her 30th birthday at the venue.

item.title: In Colombia, Anderson Cooper discovers the surprising thrills of birding
item.description: Anderson Cooper discovers how rare birds, expert guides, and wild landscapes can turn a skeptic into a passionate birder in the mountains of Colombia.

item.title: Scrutinizing the medical miracles of the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Lourdes | 60 Minutes
item.description: In 2022, 60 Minutes tracked down four living people whose unexplained medical recoveries connected to Lourdes, France have been recognized as miracles by church officials.

item.title: How "Ted Lasso" became a television phenomenon
item.description: Jason Sudeikis and the cast and crew of "Ted Lasso" tell Norah O'Donnell about the show's improbable rise and positive message.

item.title: “Ted Lasso” cast members speak about their characters
item.description: Actors James Lance, Annette Badland and Jeremy Swift talk about Trent Crimm, Mae, and Higgins, the characters they play on the award-winning show “Ted Lasso.”

item.title: Who the characters of Ted Lasso are named for
item.description: Jason Sudeikis shows 60 Minutes around the AFC Richmond locker room and tells the story of who some of the “Ted Lasso” team members are named for.
